title: KPI Definitions
description: Every KPI the system and the business track, with a PRECISE operational definition. Conforms to schema-registry.json#/definitions/kpi. The definition is the point — if two people can compute different numbers from it, it is not finished.
the_discipline: Each KPI has a `status`: measured (from real data), modeled (from a projection), target_only (a goal with no baseline), or awaiting_data (defined but empty). At TRL 3, almost everything is target_only or awaiting_data — and saying so is the honest thing. A dashboard of confident KPIs with no data behind them is theatre.
phase_note: This file now spans two different products. `phase_1_2_saas_kpis` below are the metrics for the free/premium software platform (acquisition, activation, conversion, marketplace, subscription retention). Every other section (`operational_kpis` through `impact_kpis`) is the Phase 3 hardware/monitoring layer — OmniDrone, docks, sondes, PondTwin. The Phase 1 app has zero real users as of this writing, so every Phase 1/2 KPI below is `awaiting_data`, exactly as honestly empty as the Phase 3 KPIs are. Do not let the software story's earlier launch date create pressure to backfill these with invented numbers.

phase_1_2_saas_kpis.label: Monthly Active Users (MAU)
phase_1_2_saas_kpis.definition: Distinct registered users who perform at least one tracked in-app action (open a pond profile, log a reading, view a learning module, post in community) within a rolling 30-day window.
phase_1_2_saas_kpis.formula: count(distinct user_id where last_active_at within 30 days)
phase_1_2_saas_kpis.note: The core Phase 1 acquisition-funnel metric. No app exists in production yet, so this is a definition, not a number.

phase_1_2_saas_kpis.label: DAU/MAU Ratio (Stickiness)
phase_1_2_saas_kpis.definition: Daily active users divided by monthly active users, averaged over a calendar month.
phase_1_2_saas_kpis.note: Distinguishes an app farmers check daily (feeding, logging) from one they open once and abandon. A weak ratio would be an early signal the free tier is not habitual.

phase_1_2_saas_kpis.label: Activation Rate
phase_1_2_saas_kpis.definition: Fraction of new signups who complete a defined 'activated' action set (farm profile created + at least one water-quality or feeding log entered) within 7 days of registration.
phase_1_2_saas_kpis.note: The single most important Phase 1 funnel metric — a signup who never activates was never really acquired. Precise definition matters: 'created an account' is not activation.

phase_1_2_saas_kpis.label: Free-to-Paid Conversion Rate
phase_1_2_saas_kpis.definition: Fraction of activated free-tier users who upgrade to a paid Premium subscription within a defined observation window (target: 90 days from activation).
phase_1_2_saas_kpis.note: THE monetization-thesis metric — the entire freemium business model reduces to this number times MAU times price. Nobody has paid for anything yet; treat every assumed value as fiction until a real cohort exists.

phase_1_2_saas_kpis.label: Marketplace Gross Merchandise Value (GMV)
phase_1_2_saas_kpis.definition: Total value of feed/equipment/buyer transactions conducted through the in-app marketplace in a period, before AquaVision's take-rate is deducted.
phase_1_2_saas_kpis.note: The marketplace does not exist yet. This KPI is pre-defined so that, when it launches, take-rate revenue is computed consistently against a documented GMV figure rather than an ad hoc one.

phase_1_2_saas_kpis.label: Expert Consultation Bookings
phase_1_2_saas_kpis.definition: Count of paid aquaculturist/vet consultation sessions booked and completed through the platform in a period.
phase_1_2_saas_kpis.note: Phase 2 revenue line distinct from subscription and marketplace take-rate.

phase_1_2_saas_kpis.label: Premium Subscription Churn
phase_1_2_saas_kpis.definition: Fraction of paying Premium subscribers who cancel or lapse (non-renewal) per billing period.
phase_1_2_saas_kpis.note: The SaaS-layer counterpart to kpi-churn (below), which is the Phase 3 dock/hardware-service churn metric — keep the two separate; they measure different products and will likely have very different values.

phase_1_2_saas_kpis.label: Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC)
phase_1_2_saas_kpis.definition: Fully-loaded cost (content, community, paid channels if any) to acquire one activated free-tier user, split by organic/community-led vs paid-channel acquisition.
phase_1_2_saas_kpis.note: Go-to-market for Phase 1 is explicitly organic/community-led to keep this low; this KPI is how that claim gets checked rather than assumed. See go-to-market.json.

operational_kpis.label: Survival Rate
operational_kpis.definition: Proportion of stocked individuals harvested alive, per unit per cycle.
operational_kpis.note: The headline outcome a farmer actually cares about. Our value claim ultimately reduces to moving this number. Sourced from operator harvest records, not measured by us.

operational_kpis.label: Feed Conversion Ratio
operational_kpis.definition: Mass of feed supplied divided by biomass gain, per unit per cycle.

operational_kpis.label: Diel DO Minimum
operational_kpis.definition: The lowest dissolved oxygen recorded at a unit in a 24h period, at the shallowest cage-relevant depth.
operational_kpis.note: The number nobody currently has, because it occurs pre-dawn. THE flagship measurement.

operational_kpis.label: Diel DO Amplitude
operational_kpis.definition: Difference between the daily DO maximum and minimum at a unit.
operational_kpis.note: A large amplitude signals a large algal standing crop and therefore a large nightly oxygen demand — a leading indicator a single daily reading cannot produce.

operational_kpis.label: Stratification Strength
operational_kpis.definition: Schmidt stability index normalised by column depth.

operational_kpis.label: Hypolimnetic Oxygen Debt
operational_kpis.definition: Depth-integrated oxygen deficit below the thermocline.
operational_kpis.note: Quantifies the size of the bad-water reservoir waiting to be delivered by mixing.

operational_kpis.label: Aeration Runtime
operational_kpis.definition: Total aerator operating hours per unit per period.
operational_kpis.note: The VERIFIABLE ROI anchor. Metered, already budgeted, checkable in one cycle. Lead with this. See value-proposition.json#/the_value_hierarchy.

system_health_kpis.label: Sortie Success Rate
system_health_kpis.definition: Completed sorties / attempted sorties, excluding weather scrubs.

system_health_kpis.label: Seasonal Availability
system_health_kpis.definition: Fraction of scheduled sorties actually flown, reported PER SEASON not annually.
system_health_kpis.note: MUST be reported seasonally. Availability is lowest in the monsoon, which is when risk is highest. An annual average hides exactly the gap that matters. See mission-profiles.json#/environmental_envelope.

system_health_kpis.label: Data Completeness
system_health_kpis.definition: Fraction of expected measurements that were captured, QC-passed, and stored.

system_health_kpis.label: Sensor Drift
system_health_kpis.definition: Deviation of the sonde against the laboratory reference at the weekly check, per channel.
system_health_kpis.note: The metric that catches silent fouling. See risk R-01.

system_health_kpis.label: Retrieval Calibration Quality
system_health_kpis.definition: R² and RMSE of the site-and-day-local NDCI-to-chlorophyll regression.
system_health_kpis.note: Often fitted on 3-4 points, so often poor. This KPI exists to make that honesty visible rather than hidden.

product_health_kpis.label: Alert-to-Action Rate
product_health_kpis.definition: Fraction of dispatched alerts that led to a logged operator action within a defined window.
product_health_kpis.note: A FIRST-CLASS product health metric. If it falls, people have stopped trusting the alerts and the product has failed regardless of sensor accuracy. This is the alert-fatigue early-warning signal. See data-pipeline.json.

product_health_kpis.label: False Alarm Rate
product_health_kpis.definition: Fraction of alerts a human reviewer marked as not warranting the alert.
product_health_kpis.note: Drives alert fatigue. Tune models for precision to keep this low, accepting lower recall.

product_health_kpis.label: Anomaly Detection Precision
product_health_kpis.definition: True positives / (true positives + false positives), on human-reviewed detections.
product_health_kpis.note: Precision is prioritised over recall here. See ai-ml-models.json.

business_kpis.label: Stations per Dock
business_kpis.definition: Active sampling stations served by one dock.
business_kpis.note: The dominant driver of unit economics. See unit-economics.json.

business_kpis.label: Contribution Margin per Dock
business_kpis.definition: Revenue per dock minus cost-to-serve per dock, per period.
business_kpis.note: Cannot be computed until real BOM and maintenance costs and a real price exist. Deliberately empty rather than modeled.

business_kpis.label: Maintenance Labour per Dock
business_kpis.definition: Field-labour hours per dock per period.
business_kpis.note: The most-underestimated cost line. See risk R-14.

business_kpis.label: Customer Churn
business_kpis.definition: Fraction of customers not renewing per period.
business_kpis.note: Phase 3 hardware/monitoring-service churn specifically — commercial/enterprise customers renting dock capacity. Expected low once trust is established (data lock-in, hardware in our docks), but unproven. Do not confuse with kpi-subscription-churn (phase_1_2_saas_kpis), which is the Premium software-subscription churn — a different customer base and likely a different rate.

impact_kpis.label: Mortality Events Mitigated
impact_kpis.definition: Documented cases where a warning led to an intervention that a farmer judged prevented or reduced a loss.
impact_kpis.note: A COUNTERFACTUAL and therefore hard to measure honestly. Requires the methodology in methodology.json. Never claim a prevented kill without the evidence structure to support it.

impact_kpis.label: Dangerous Boat Trips Avoided
impact_kpis.definition: Pre-dawn/bad-weather manual monitoring trips replaced by autonomous sorties.
impact_kpis.note: The occupational-safety impact. Concrete and measurable.

impact_kpis.label: Smallholder Reach
impact_kpis.definition: Smallholder operators served (via cooperatives/programmes) as a fraction of total served.
impact_kpis.note: The equity metric. Measures whether the mission is being served or quietly abandoned in favour of the payers. See social-economic-impact.json.

the_meta_point.statement: Almost every KPI here is status awaiting_data or target_only — the Phase 1/2 SaaS KPIs included, not just the Phase 3 hardware ones.
the_meta_point.why_that_is_correct: At TRL 3 there is no real hardware data, and the Phase 1 app has zero real users, so honest KPIs are empty on both sides of the business. A dashboard showing confident values for either set would be fabricating them. The value of this file today is that it DEFINES each metric precisely, so that the moment real data arrives — whether that is the first app signup or the first drone sortie — everyone computes the same number, and nobody can quietly redefine a KPI to make it look better. Pre-committing the definitions is a discipline, not a limitation.
